text: I prefer to live in a very safe place, even if it means having less freedom. For example: I accept cameras everywhere, curfews at night, or showing ID to enter certain areas.
I don't mind if the government checks my messages and calls to fight crime. For example: if police want to read my texts without permission, that's fine - I have nothing to hide.
Traitors deserve the worst punishments. For example: anyone who shares military secrets or helps enemies should get life in prison or death penalty.
A society only works with clear hierarchy and obedience. For example: soldiers must obey officers, children must respect parents, workers must follow bosses.
In a big crisis, the government should have special powers, even if some rights are suspended. For example: during a pandemic or war, I accept lockdowns or censorship.
Those who disrespect national symbols don't deserve to live here. For example: anyone who burns the flag or insults the anthem should lose citizenship.
A country is only respected if it has powerful armed forces. For example: we should spend a lot on tanks and jets, even if we cut other things.
A good criminal is one in jail - or worse. For example: I support longer sentences and more police freedom, even if they make mistakes sometimes.
Frequent changes in government only slow down the country. For example: it would be better to have a strong leader for 10-20 years to make big changes.
The safety of good citizens must come before criminal rights. For example: if a suspect dies during a police operation, I see no problem.
Illegal immigrants should be sent back right away, no exceptions. For example: even if they've been here for years or have kids here - they must go.
Military service should be longer and harder. For example: every young person should serve at least 2 years, learning discipline and love for country.
Protests should have stricter limits. For example: blocking streets or strikes that affect hospitals should mean jail time.
Police should be able to use deadly force more easily. For example: if a suspect runs or fights back, the officer should shoot without fear of punishment.
National borders are sacred and must be protected at any cost. For example: we should build walls and use military tech to stop all illegal entry.
Good citizens should have full right to carry guns. For example: anyone without a criminal record should be able to buy and carry a gun.
Loyalty to your own country must come before any global cause. For example: we must defend our country first, even against international rules.
Schools should teach more patriotism and respect for authority. For example: singing the anthem weekly and studying national heroes.
Extremist groups should be watched by police. For example: radical groups should have undercover agents to stop threats before they happen.
The death penalty should exist for terrible crimes. For example: child abusers, serial killers, or terrorists should be executed.
The government should be able to block websites that threaten public order. For example: sites that spread fake news or organize illegal protests should be shut down quickly.
Prisons should be harder, not more comfortable. For example: no TV, no gym, no visits. Criminals must pay for what they did.
In case of war, everyone should help. For example: if attacked, every able citizen should fight or work in defense, with no option to refuse.
National identity is being destroyed by globalization. For example: we are losing our culture because of foreign influences and big companies.
Strong leaders are better than politicians who keep making deals. For example: I prefer a president who makes quick firm decisions, even if controversial.
Respect for flag and anthem should be required by law. For example: disrespecting these symbols should mean fines or even jail.
The traditional family is the base of the nation and must be protected. For example: the government should give benefits to traditional marriages with children.
International organizations like the UN interfere too much. For example: they keep criticizing us - we should ignore them or leave.
Order and discipline are more important than creativity and freedom. For example: an organized society where everyone knows their place works better.
War veterans and police deserve special status. For example: those who risk their lives should get higher retirement and public recognition.
The government only slows down the economy - less interference is better. For example: companies should hire and fire freely, without strict labor laws.
Those who risk everything to start a business deserve to get rich. For example: if someone quit their job and worked 16 hours a day to succeed, they earned every cent.
High taxes are basically legal theft. For example: it's unfair for the government to take 30-40% of my salary for bureaucracy and corrupt politicians.
If you're poor, it's mostly your own fault. For example: there are stories of people who came from nothing and got rich - those who work hard can succeed.
The free market solves problems better than the government. For example: private hospitals work better than public ones; Uber is better than regulated taxis.
Each person is responsible for their own success or failure. For example: blaming the system or racism is just an excuse from those who don't want to work.
There should be no limit to how rich someone can get. For example: if Elon Musk made 200 billion, it's because he created value - government shouldn't limit that.
Too many labor laws do more harm than good. For example: strict rules make companies inflexible and protect bad employees.
Billionaires are heroes who drive the economy. For example: without entrepreneurs, millions wouldn't have jobs - we should admire them.
Politicians don't understand economics and should interfere less. For example: decisions about money should be made by economists, not legislators.
Privatizing state companies is always the best solution. For example: postal services and banks would work better if sold to private business.
Unions are mafias that only slow down workers. For example: they strike for any reason and their leaders get rich from worker fees.
Minimum wage should be set by the market, not government. For example: if government forces higher pay, companies just won't hire.
Welfare programs create dependency and laziness. For example: they make people prefer to stay home getting money rather than work.
Meritocracy is the fairest system. For example: those who study and work more deserve to earn more - no need to level everyone down for equality.
Inheritances shouldn't be taxed - the money was already taxed once. For example: if my father worked and paid taxes, government shouldn't take part of what he left me.
Competition always improves products and services. For example: when companies compete, prices go down and quality goes up.
Too many environmental rules slow down development. For example: we have land and resources but can't use them because of rules made by foreign groups.
Education should be a personal investment, not a government job. For example: those who want to study should pay - others shouldn't fund their college.
Entrepreneurs take risks that employees don't, so they deserve much more pay. For example: if the company fails, the owner loses everything.
Inequality is natural and even needed for progress. For example: if everyone earned the same, no one would try harder or innovate.
Private health plans are better than public ones. For example: those who can pay should leave public healthcare - more would be left for those who need it.
Profit is the best sign that a company is doing something right. For example: if a company makes profit, it's delivering what people want.
Rich countries got rich through merit, not exploitation. For example: USA and Japan are rich because of hard work and education, not stealing.
Free international trade benefits everyone. For example: when countries trade freely, each makes what they're best at and everyone gets better products.
Startups show that anyone can get rich with a good idea. For example: Zuckerberg made Facebook in his dorm room - opportunity exists for the brave.
Government should run like a company: lean and efficient. For example: cut costs, fire unproductive workers, outsource services.
Private charity is more efficient than government programs. For example: when you donate directly, money reaches those in need - through government, it disappears.
Rich people who donate do more good than any tax. For example: Bill Gates with his foundation saved more lives than many governments.
Business failure is part of learning and shouldn't be punished. For example: many successful entrepreneurs failed many times before getting it right.
Without God or some faith, life has no real meaning. For example: atheists may seem happy, but only spirituality gives real purpose to life.
There is a moral law higher than human laws. For example: even if abortion is legal, it's still wrong in God's eyes - divine law is worth more.
The wisdom of religious traditions is worth more than modern trends. For example: traditional marriage and Christian values worked for 2000 years - don't abandon them.
Those who practice spirituality have a peace that money doesn't give. For example: people who pray or go to church are more balanced than those who only seek wealth.
Moral relativism is destroying society. For example: when we say everyone has their own truth, we open the door to all kinds of decay.
Families that practice faith together are more united. For example: going to church every Sunday and praying before meals creates a stable home.
No matter how successful someone is, without faith something is missing. For example: rich celebrities who get depressed prove that money doesn't fill the void.
In the hardest moments of life, only faith truly helps. For example: when someone loses a child or gets a bad diagnosis, belief in something greater saves them.
Spiritual experiences like miracles and answered prayers are real. For example: I know people who were healed after prayers - those who deny never felt God's power.
Spiritual values protect against modern temptations. For example: young people in church are less likely to use drugs or commit crime.
Science can't explain everything - some mysteries only faith can reach. For example: where did the universe come from? What happens after death? Religion has answers.
Society was better when religion had more influence. For example: in the past there was more respect, less violence, more stable families.
Faith gives strength to overcome any problem. For example: people with strong faith face illness and loss with more courage than those who believe nothing.
Moral and religious education should be part of school. For example: children should learn ethical values and the importance of spirituality.
Marriage is sacred, not just a legal contract. For example: it's a union blessed by God - easy divorce weakens this institution.
Human life is sacred from conception to natural death. For example: abortion and euthanasia are wrong because only God decides when life begins or ends.
Gratitude and humility are more important than personal success. For example: a humble person, even if poor, lives happier than a proud ambitious one.
Religious communities offer belonging that social media never will. For example: in church you find real support and true friendships.
Forgiveness is more powerful than revenge. For example: holding grudges only hurts yourself - forgiving brings inner peace.
Nature is a divine creation that we must care for with respect. For example: the perfection of ecosystems proves a creative intelligence - not random chance.
Spiritual practices like meditation and prayer improve mental health. For example: studies show religious people have less anxiety and depression.
Death is not the end, but a passage to another life. For example: believing in afterlife gives comfort and removes fear of death.
The Ten Commandments are the foundation of any civilized society. For example: don't kill, don't steal, don't lie - these should guide all laws.
Faith helps me accept what I cannot change. For example: when something bad happens, understanding it's part of a bigger plan gives me peace.
Love for others is the most important rule. For example: helping those in need and being generous - these are true marks of a good person.
Modesty and simplicity are forgotten values today. For example: the culture of showing off and selfies goes against spiritual humility.
Religious leaders have special wisdom to guide people. For example: priests and pastors dedicate their lives to spiritual study - their advice is valuable.
Religious rituals and holidays give rhythm and meaning to life. For example: Christmas, Easter - these dates connect us with something greater.
Conscience is the voice of God within us. For example: that feeling of guilt when we do wrong or peace when we do right - that's our spiritual guide.
The spiritual quest is what makes humans different from animals. For example: we are the only beings who question the meaning of life and seek transcendence.
